description: Very positive. A helpful tool to get teams to comply with advanced security features that are necessary in today's World.
author: Chris H.
pros: The integration into all aspects of my daily functions. I can maintain extremely strong passwords, simply
cons: The integration with some apps forces a 2FA code, but going to retrieve the code closes integration.

review_title: You will not regret this software!
description: 10/10, 5 stars, all the ratings - the best product on the market by a long shot - plus they sponsor [sensitive content hidden] F1 so that is pleasing also.
author: Victoria P.
pros: It is easy to install, easy to learn to use, the browser extensions are awesome, the ability to securely share passwords with users is unbelievably helpful in team environments and the search functions work super well. The sales team are really lovely and not pushy and happy to make sure you have the right product for you, not constantly trying to upsell.
cons: While I appreciate the added security of not being able to do a forgot password on a master password, it would be helpful to have some sort of opt-in feature for business based accounts for admins to force that for the inevitable person who will forget the password and learn the hard way that they need to start from scratch, as that can be a time drain. They always only need to learn once, but its a time consuming lesson!

description: No issues and the the Security audit feature really helps calling out any passwords that should be changed to increase security.
author: James H.
pros: Ease of use, value for the money and the sharing feature is great for IT who do password resets and want a secure way to share with staff.
cons: Would like if it included a note taking feature like 1Password provides. The only hiccup I've experienced is it doesn't recognize certain websites - or should say it misidentifies .. but a page refresh fixes.

description: It was good, until they price hiked nearly 50%. We used to have what felt like a great discount, but after getting us on the hook, and a few rep changes, they no longer negotiate prices. We have had 4 reps, in almost 3 years.
author: Ali R.
pros: It's a good software, works as intended, new features added often. iphone, android, and windows apps, browser extension.
cons: Seems there are semi constant price hikes, changes in reps, and feels like a cash grab to loyal customers. We came to keeper due to pricing; they raise the price almost 10% yearly. This is not including discounts they also removed.

description: Overall experience is good. Admins and users have found their way to work with the tool and they can administrate their own groups / teams.
author: Jan P.
pros: Ease of use for admins and users. Multiplatform (Desktop, phone, browser tools), Good value for money. Great sharing possibilities
cons: User adoption is always internal process, there is no reminder for unused invitations and no one forces users to activate licences.

description: My overall experience with Keeper has been outstanding. The interface is intuitive, making it easy to manage passwords and secure files. Customer support has been exceptional, always ready to assist with any questions or issues. It's well documented as well, one of the best documentations in my life. Really. The reliability and performance of Keeper Security have exceeded my expectations, making it an essential tool for my digital security needs. I highly recommend Keeper Security to anyone looking for a robust and user-friendly security solution.
author: Andrey S.
pros: The most. Hmm.... I can definitely highlight the user-friendly interface and easy to navigate, high level of security and encryption, customer support and quick response times, wide range of features including secure file storage and password management, reliable and consistent performance. As well as license management flexibility
cons: Some advanced features have a bit of a learning curve. For example, I remember that SSO integration was a bit unclear, but it works in the end.

review_title: Keeper Security is great, Keeper Connections Manager is not.
description: Pretty good. Again - Keeper Security has been easy to use and great implementation, our issue is with KCM and having paid for a service we can't use.
author: Samantha C.
pros: Simple and easy password management. Easy to share passwords in a very secure way with employees who need access to portals but don't need to know the log-in information.
cons: Keeper Security is great, but KCM is something we enrolled in and haven't been able to use it at all. We were sold on it's ability to provide highly managed and recorded sign-ons, and we discussed which websites and programs we would use it for, and then it turned out to be too complicated for their techs to make work. A waste of lots of time and money.

description: Keeper was a seamless solution when LastPass blew up in our faces. The business relationship is simple, the product is of high quality and my team uses it as intended. For a password manager, that's my biggest ask. We aren't doing anything with PAM or automated changes today but those capabilities exist when we're ready for them.
author: Scott H.
pros: It is inexpensive, very easy to use and very secure. Between the mobile app and browser plug-in, I'm able to easily fill in passwords without the risk posed by auto-fill from other vendors. Sharing and organizing secrets is also simple with support for group memberships and shared folders.
cons: Nothing comes to mind. We've turned on some of their advanced security features like device approval and still no real issues.

review_title: Very good but room for improvement
description: The user experience is better than LastPass, but worse than Bitwarden.
author: Olly B.
pros: One-time sharing feature very useful Sharing folders with teams and adjusting permissions works very well Great audit statistics Relatively good user interface and user experience
cons: Browser extension quite cramped and no way to expand No keyboard shortcut to fill matching credentials (Bitwarden has cmd+shift+L) No option to set how a website URL is matched (Bitwarden has several options) Autofill button injected into form fields often difficult or impossible to click, depending on the website's design

description: The UI and brand overall is a rising star in the industry with a bunch of security features. A+ Exp.
author: Aleks H.
pros: Password and login management features is a nice touch. Compromised credentials in BreachWatch is a nice add-on in the security sense.
cons: Didn't get a walk through upon purchase. Learned on the go. Could use some more color coding options for created record types.

description: We used this for all customer passwords so the engineers who needs to have access to the customer passwords get access based on Azure Groups, it's always safe with them!
author: Bart H.
pros: Browser plugin is very easy, app on Windows is also very easy to use. Good quality, good price, Azure integration, MFA based on SSO with Office 365, secure data storage
cons: Nothing, it works fine for us and we save all password in it

description: I have no reason to doubt Keeper from a security point of view, and it's serviceable from a usability point of view, so it's okay overall. For it to become a truly exceptional tool, it would need to add the missing features that competitors have and tweak the UI a bit so that it's a bit more modern. (Like, can we get rid of the "Encryption Enabled" message? It's silly to think a password manager wouldn't use encryption in this day and age.)
author: Justin S.
pros: Keeper is reasonably priced compared to the competitors we considered. Unlike virtually all SaaS vendors, they publish detailed release notes for all of their component products, which is *super* nice. (I just wish they included RSS feeds with those release notes so I didn't have to make my own...) Keeper is compatible with many different platforms, including Linux (Keeper desktop) and Safari (Keeper browser extension). A lot of competitors couldn't give a whit about products like Linux and Safari.
cons: It's been years since we signed up with Keeper, but it still has some fairly basic limitations that it needs to overcome. For instance, in shared folders, it'd be nice to have separate sharing settings for subfolders. The UI as a whole is just not as refined as something like 1Password.

review_title: Keeper is great for individual or group use
description: Keeper is the best. I like the interface a lot and I am probably going to keep using it for a while.
author: Arka R.
pros: Keeper is the first password manager I've used and likely will be the last. I like being able to organize strong passwords and share them securely
cons: It can be difficult to authorize additional devices for a web vault

description: its been great both professionally and privately
author: Zhaklina A.
pros: ease of use and the fact that it can be used across all devices
cons: that it automatically pops up all the time. i want to be able to control the auto fill easier

description: We've used for over a year and had a very positive experience!
author: Diane U.
pros: I don't have to think about any of these details after I enter them - so easy to use for that reason and to keep our org safer!
cons: Sometimes the direct launch link that was entered "breaks" from the actual launch...not sure why, but at least the info is still safely saved.

review_title: Loved this would totally go back
author: Jennifer K.
pros: I loved everything it made logging into websites so much easier and less stressful.
cons: I have to admit I fought using it in the beginning but when my boss took it away I was very upset!

review_title: Best money a small business can spend
description: I consider Keeper the first and best weapon in our cybersecurity strategy. As a small business with remote employees, we rely on the product daily to manage access to myriad systems. This platform allows me oversight and easy adoption, while providing all of the features I would expect from a top shelf security product.
author: Jonathan D.
pros: We have found Keeper to be an easy and effective way for us to manage our company's password standards and security. Easy to share records with employees and generate passwords and eliminate reuse. I also love the zero-knowledge platform - our employees feel comfortable knowing they can use if for their personal passwords also, which helped with adoption.
cons: Some of the browser extensions were a bit buggy in the beginning but they have been sorted out. The same with the new iPhone app. Nothing huge and all were corrected within an appropriate amount of time.

review_title: Keeper Security Saved Me After A Robbery
description: I currently use the Keeper Security app on my iPhone 6 and my MacBook Pro. Nearly four years ago I was struggling to keep track of all of my log in IDs and passwords. I knew it was important to have a variety of passwords and a complexity to them across all the electronic areas I utilized. I found the Keeper Security app to be easy to use. I soon out-grew my need for the free version and purchased the full application. This product has far surpassed my expectations! It is easy to use, back up, and synchronize with my laptop. I highly recommend this product for individuals, but would recommend it for small businesses as well. A final testimony to how this product has served me: In 2015 my home was broken into and my spouse and I got cleaned out! To make matters more complicated, we were living outside of the USA at the time. Luckily, I still had one computer and I was able to access my Keeper Security backup. Because I had Keeper I could provide all of the account numbers of my banking information, license, and passport to the police. I could call back to the USA and work with one of my banks and my credit card institutions, because I had kept the phone numbers associated with them in the Keeper Security folders that I set up. This application, this software really saved me during one of my darkest days. Again, I highly recommend this program.
author: Catherine A. M.
pros: Easy to use. Easy to access when you need to look up something quickly and in the moment. I have 235 listings currently and I grow the data base each week because I find one more thing to keep secure. I am able to have highly complex passwords for my financial profiles because Keeper will generate them for me. I can choose the length of password I need and press the magic dice! I like having all of my insurance information, email passwords, all the access information I forget to use with Apple, and I can have a different password for each website I sign into.
cons: The one thing that I have recommended to Keeper is to create a price break for those who want it for just two people in the household. Right now there is no price break for two. The pricing structure is great if you want to buy a family plan, but I don't have five people in my household.

review_title: Never forget a login or lose a password again. Even those that are seldom used.
author: Patrick L.
pros: Easy to use app. The new user quickly realizes what a functional tool Keeper is for organizing in logical fashion all you logins and passwords. Folders allow for keeping track of multiple accounts related to the same login. There is the option to leave comments on the app directly inside the folder so that "thing" you have to remember you don't forget. The app allows the addition of fields to expand on the size and amount of data you can enter into the folder. If you have several items that you discover need to be put into one folder you can do this quite easily. I have come to rely on this app and not my memory as the number of logins and passwords in my life grow. The cost is minimal but the value is evident. It is a simple idea that was well thought out and does what it claims to do. What more can you ask from a product? I gave customer support 4 stars only because I have never had to use them for any reason. If service is like the product in terms of reliability than it would have been correct to give them 5 stars. I was skeptical about this product in he beginning because there are so many competitors I thought it was a risky buy. It was a good purchase. I don't love the product it just works!
cons: The only complaint it isn't free. I have tried other free password vaults and they fall short of Keeper. I know there should be more to complain about but I haven't found anything.
author_profession: Enrolled Agent - Certified Tax Resolution Specialist

review_title: Simple, Elegant Password Management that I have used for 2+ years
description: I have used the app for over two years and have found it to be a time-efficient and safe way to store my passwords for ALL my online accounts. I also store other important information on there (like SS # etc)
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Browser App Extension allows you to have passwords automatically entered. Such a time saver Seamless integration and syncing between devices I guess we have no way of knowing if the App is REALLY safe but if you goto keeper.com they have extensive documentation on how they encrypt and store your data, their security disclosure and their certifications. I guess if you're really worried you should store your passwords under your mattress at home.
cons: Sometimes the Browser App Extension Popup does not bring up the correct login. An example of this occurs when you are on an ecommerce site and taken to a 3rd party payment site (ie Paypal Popup) but you retain the shops URL. As Keeper does URL matching to determine what password to retrieve this can be an issue...you need to open the Vault interface and get your paypal password. A way to select what username/password combo from the popup dialog would make things easier.

description: I really love the product and I recommend this to my colleagues. I even helped in the implementation of this application company-wide. Before, we were using it for the management level, but now, it is now used in all levels promoting data security and protecting client's data by providing masked logins to operation staff.
author: Grietz R.
pros: I love that it has the ability to set the access of a user and apply privacy screen. I also love the Breachwatch function that alerts us if there are passwords that are weak and those that need updating.
cons: It's not that I like it least but I would love it if it's cheaper so it's available to anyone. Pricing.

review_title: Great for enterprise, small business, community organisations or personal use
description: - I love that I can attach files to a record. Example of use: saving a login and password for an insurance site allows me to keep images of my possessions attached to that site. - I use Keeper's random password generator when I am signing up to a new site. This is so handy as I don't have to spend ages finding a password that conforms to organisational complexity. - Business use: I keep my website domain hosting details in Keeper. Create a record, enter the login and password but also attach notes to the record such as renewal dates, which credit card I used to renew, domain lookup information.... pretty much anything I need to know about renewing my domain is cut and pasted into this record for easy access. - Personal use: As I am a tech support for my family and friends, I have a folder for Mum, one for Dad, one for Best Friend etc. As they are all technophobic, i keep their logins and passwords as a backup so if they forget, they can just call me and ask.
author: Sharyn M.
pros: The feature that I like best about Keeper is being able to access it anytime, anywhere from any device - at the time that I need it. I've used it for both work and personal use and recommend it to all my customers. - I only have to remember one password - it only takes me a second to access any site. - I have the ability to copy and paste a password without exposing the password to others to see. - Keeper has a browser extension that allows me to save new site logins and passwords automatically. - I like that I can sort passwords into Subjects or Folders, which is easier for organisation
cons: I would prefer the ability to make sub-folders under the main folder headings, but that is not a show-stopper - more of a personal preference really. I think the pricing is reasonable, however, I think the personal licence should include two devices instead of just one.

author: Lynn W.
pros: Dual Factor authentication Active Directory integration is an absolute plus Adding sites and passwords is intuitive and simple Great Customer service
cons: To be honest, so far I have not found any. The product thus far has been great. Our users love it, even our users that are not very tech savvy seem to like it.

author: Veronica D.
pros: I have been using this app for at least 5 years, possibly longer. I pay the low-end fee, once per year. I didn't do that for the first couple years. I decided to pay though because I can now use it on ANY device and it transfers with me. We need to remember so many sites and passwords that it becomes a big headache! It has saved me soooo many times! I have made two folders for my two children and in them I have stored websites and passwords just for them. That has been so helpful!
cons: It has a function in which you can automatically be sent to save a new password for a new website/app. It would be nice so that you don't ever forget to save a password... it has never worked correctly for me. But, it doesn't bother me much.. Both Google and Samsung ask to save new passwords anyway, so they are backups..

description: Using it for several years and I'm very happy with it.
author: Nathanael P.
pros: Meets the standards set by the Commonwealth of MA to keep client information safe
cons: I'm just a user so I don't really know how much it costs
